strengths: Accessibility-first design: Okabe-Ito palette combined with distinct hatch patterns ensures the figure is readable for colorblind readers and in greyscale print.
Robust font-embedding pipeline: pdf.fonttype=42 in rcParams plus a post-save pdffonts verification guard guarantees editable, non-bitmap text in the PDF.
weaknesses: figure.png could not be visually inspected in this review environment, so pixel-level label overlap or bar readability at 300 dpi cannot be independently confirmed.
No explicit data.json is present in the output directory, so the exact numeric values underlying the caption's claims (+13.1 pp, +12.9 pp) cannot be cross-checked against the source data.

evidence.scientific_clarity: Grouped bar chart with 4 methods × 5 benchmarks, error bars shown, OursModel visually dominant; brief's claim (highlight overall winner) is directly addressed.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' set in rcParams; Arial embedded as CID TrueType (confirmed by pdffonts); font stack avoids DejaVu Sans default; 8–9 pt sizes appropriate for single-column.
evidence.palette: Explicit Okabe-Ito palette (grey/sky-blue/amber/strong-blue); hatch patterns added for B&W legibility; no red/green used for directional meaning — all categorical.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(6.5, 3.6) single-column friendly; y-range starts at max(0, min−5) per brief hint (not forced to 0); tight_layout pad=0.5; 2-column legend inside axes saves space.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure exactly; specific gains cited (+13.1 pp MATH, +12.9 pp GPQA); legend inside axes; error bars and n=5 seeds documented; color+hatch noted for greyscale.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts confirms CID TrueType (ArialMT), embedded, no Type 3 fonts; script includes post-save Type-3 check that exits non-zero on failure.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (73 KB), figure.png (142 KB), figure.svg (318 KB), caption.tex (517 B), gen_figure.py (5.2 KB) all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states the finding ('OursModel leads on all five tasks'), gives two specific numerical claims (+13.1 pp, +12.9 pp), explains error bars (mean ± std, n=5 seeds), and lists all five benchmarks.

strengths: Colorblind-safe Okabe-Ito palette with no green/red ambiguity and clear blue accent for the proposed method.
Robust vector font pipeline: pdf.fonttype=42 in rcParams plus runtime pdffonts verification prevents Type-3 bitmap fonts.
weaknesses: Gap annotation at final step only marks the distance to Method-A; the larger gap to Baseline is stated in the caption but not visually annotated in the figure.
DejaVu Sans appears as third fallback in the font stack — while Arial/Helvetica are preferred, environments missing those fonts would silently fall back to the non-preferred default.

strengths: Excellent typography: Arial font family, pdf.fonttype=42 enforced and verified with pdffonts, no bitmap fonts – fully editable vector PDF.
Caption is publication-ready: clearly describes encoding, highlights the specific numerical finding (diagonal 69–92), and states sample size (N=1000).
weaknesses: Palette receives a minor deduction: while Blues is colorblind-safe and appropriate, white grid lines on very dark cells can occasionally obscure cell boundaries at the highest normalised values.
SVG is generated but was not required by the brief; the script's pdffonts check adds robustness but also a hard sys.exit(2) dependency that would fail silently if pdffonts is not installed.

evidence.scientific_clarity: 10x10 confusion matrix is clearly visible with diagonal annotations in bold white and off-diagonal in grey; row normalisation shown via Blues colormap with colorbar; axes labelled 'True class' / 'Predicted class' matching the brief exactly.

evidence.palette: Uses sequential Blues colormap (perceptually uniform, colorblind-safe) which is correct for a normalised confusion matrix; no problematic red/green direction encoding; minor grid lines are white-on-blue which is legible but could clash at very low values – minor concern only.
evidence.layout_density: 7×6 inch figure with tight_layout(); 10×10 grid fills the axes well; colorbar adds right margin cleanly; no gaping whitespace or overlapping labels observed; cell annotations skip zeros to reduce clutter.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content: colormap type (Blues), row-normalisation, raw counts on cells, and N=1000 are all stated; bold diagonal range (69–92) is explicitly cited; colorbar label is informative; legend is not needed here and correctly absent.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts shows CID TrueType embedded fonts (Arial-BoldMT, ArialMT) with no Type-3 raster fonts; script explicitly checks for /Type3 and Type-3 in pdffonts output and exits with error if found; SVG saved with svg.fonttype='none' for editability.

strengths: Complete, publication-ready figure with all required files, correct colorblind-safe Okabe-Ito palette, and proper vector font settings (pdf.fonttype=42).
Caption is exemplary: contains a specific quantitative finding, describes every visual element, and defines statistical notation—ready for submission without revision.
weaknesses: Minor: significance bracket for Drug-A 10µM is labeled 'ns' but the p-value is dynamically computed—if the simulated data happen to yield p<0.05 the label would mismatch the caption claim; a runtime check is included but the hard-coded 'ns' default in the comparisons list could mislead.
Minor: jitter width (±0.18) slightly exceeds the box half-width (±0.12), so scattered points can visually overlap neighboring violin bodies at high density—a narrower jitter (±0.12) would be cleaner.

evidence.scientific_clarity: Violin+box+jitter layout clearly shows dose-dependent viability reduction Control→Drug-A 10µM→50µM→Combo; medians and distributions are unambiguous; significance brackets computed via Mann-Whitney U and rendered correctly.

strengths: Exemplary font hygiene: pdf.fonttype=42 enforced, Arial embedded as CID TrueType confirmed by pdffonts, with automated Type-3 guard in the script.

strengths: Perfect vector output with embedded Arial TrueType fonts (pdf.fonttype=42, no Type 3), with an automated runtime font-type verification step in the generation script.

strengths: Exemplary typography setup: pdf.fonttype=42, svg.fonttype=none, Arial-first font stack, and pdffonts-verified CID TrueType embedding with no Type 3 fonts.
Colorblind-safe single-hue sequential blue palette is well-matched to the ordered cluster data, and all required output files are present and non-trivial.
weaknesses: OVERLAP variable is misimplemented: SCALE=STEP*(1-OVERLAP)/max_density caps ridge height at 0.45×STEP so ridges are separated by gaps, not overlapping — contradicting both the brief and the caption.
Caption's 'mild vertical overlap' claim is factually incorrect per the code geometry, which would draw immediate scrutiny from a reviewer and undermines trust in the other numerical claims.

evidence.scientific_clarity: 6-cluster ridgeline with progressive shift claim is present, but OVERLAP=0.55 is misimplemented so ridges have 0.55-STEP gaps rather than the overlap the brief requires.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' set in rcParams; Arial leads the font stack; pdffonts confirms CID TrueType embedded/subset/unicode — no DejaVu default, no Type 3.
evidence.palette: Single-hue sequential blue (BLUE_PALE→BLUE_DEEP) is colorblind-safe and encodes cluster order correctly; no red/green directional misuse.
evidence.layout_density: SCALE=STEP*(1-OVERLAP)/max_density caps ridge height at 0.45×STEP, leaving a 0.55-STEP gap between ridges; x-range trimmed to 0.5–99.5 pct and tight_layout used, but vertical space is wasted.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption asserts 'mild vertical overlap' but code geometry produces gapped ridges — Reviewer 2 would flag this mismatch; numerical claims and methodology description are otherwise solid.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is a real PDF v1.4 (47 KB); pdffonts shows one CID TrueType font (ArialMT), embedded, subset, unicode — no Type 3 bitmap fonts.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (47 KB), figure.png (83 KB), figure.svg (137 KB), caption.tex (13 lines), gen_figure.py (144 lines) all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Includes specific numerical claims (median ~3.3 vs ~2.0, N=300–500), Scott's bandwidth, and color encoding explanation, but 'mild vertical overlap' is factually wrong given the code's geometry.

evidence.scientific_clarity: Dual-axis layout matches brief exactly: training loss on log-scale left axis, cosine LR schedule on right axis. Both axes color-coupled to their lines. Log scale applied via ax1.set_yscale('log'). Caption confirms monotonic loss decrease (2.19→0.21) and LR decay (1e-3→0). No directional ambiguity introduced.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and ps.fonttype=42 set in rcParams; svg.fonttype='none' also set. Font family is a clean sans-serif stack (Arial → Helvetica → DejaVu Sans → Liberation Sans). Font sizes are consistent: axis labels 9pt, tick labels 8pt, legend 8pt. Post-save pdffonts check in script actively validates no Type-3 fonts escaped.
evidence.palette: COLOR_LOSS=#0F4D92 (blue) and COLOR_LR=#D55E00 (orange-red). This is the Wong/IBM colorblind-safe pair. Both lines are monotone-decreasing so directional confusion is avoided; brief hint about 'more is better' ambiguity is respected. Spine, tick, and label colors are all set to match each line.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(7,4), tight_layout() and bbox_inches='tight' applied. Top and right spines disabled on ax1; ax2 shows only right spine. No legend box frame (legend.frameon=False). Tick direction 'out' with minimal length (2.2). Legend placed upper-right inside axes where data is sparse. No clutter or wasted whitespace apparent.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content precisely. Numerical claims (~2.19→~0.21 loss, 1e-3→0 LR) are specific and verifiable from data.json. Caption explicitly states 'no values were mocked' and cites data source. Color and axis assignments described textually, removing any ambiguity for a reader seeing only the PDF.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdf.fonttype=42 (TrueType embedding) confirmed in rcParams. Script runs pdffonts post-save and exits with code 2 if Type-3 fonts are detected; falls back to raw byte-scan if pdffonts is unavailable. figure.svg also saved with svg.fonttype='none' (text as paths). Three-layer verification ensures no bitmap font regression.
evidence.file_format: All four required outputs present: figure.pdf, figure.png (300 dpi), caption.tex, gen_figure.py. Script also emits figure.svg as a bonus. figure.png saved at dpi=300, suitable for print. No required file is missing.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption is a single well-formed LaTeX \caption{}. It names both series, their axis assignments, and scale type (log). Quantitative findings stated with appropriate precision (~2.19, ~0.21, 1e-3). Encoding strategy (color-coupled axes/spines/labels) described. Data provenance declared. No jargon or claims absent from the figure.

strengths: Exemplary technical rigor: Okabe-Ito palette, pdf.fonttype=42, embedded Arial, and a post-save font verification step all in one script.

No explicit data labels or error indicators inside the bars; for N=1 this is acceptable, but a reviewer might request at least visible segment boundaries.

Minor palette-accessibility gap aside, the layout has modest unused whitespace in the lower panel area that could be tightened by reducing figure height or increasing SCALE.

evidence.scientific_clarity: Sankey diagram clearly traces 1,120 raw samples through cleaning, filtering, and final splits; node labels with n-values and proportional band widths make the flow immediately readable.

strengths: Exemplary font handling: pdf.fonttype=42 + svg.fonttype='none' set globally, font stack prefers system sans-serif, verified no Type3 bitmap fonts in PDF output.
Caption is scientifically complete with quantitative claim (235 up, 230 down), threshold values, axis definitions, and honest caveat about index-based gene IDs.
weaknesses: Scatter points are rasterized (rasterized=True) in the PDF, so individual data points are not selectable/scalable vectors — acceptable for 3000-point density but worth noting for strict vector-only requirements.
Gene annotation offsets are fixed constants (±0.25 x, +0.18 y) which may cause label overlap for top-5 genes clustered at similar FC values; no repel logic is used.

evidence.scientific_clarity: Volcano plot clearly encodes the brief's up/down/ns classification with threshold lines, count annotations in corners, and top-5 gene labels — claim is immediately legible.
evidence.typography: Script sets pdf.fonttype=42, svg.fonttype='none', and font stack Arial→Helvetica→DejaVu Sans; no Type3 fonts in PDF (CIDFontType2 confirmed); axis/tick/legend text sizes are differentiated (6.5–10 pt).
evidence.palette: Red (#C0392B) = up, blue (#2471A3) = down, grey (#AAAAAA) = ns — directional colors reserved for direction, neutral grey for non-sig; palette is colorblind-distinguishable (red/blue/grey are Okabe–Ito-safe).
evidence.layout_density: 6×5 in figure fills space well; legend placed below axes via bbox_to_anchor to avoid overplotting; count annotations in upper corners; y-range auto-fits data with no excessive whitespace observed in PNG.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content exactly (235 up, 230 down, thresholds stated); legend is outside plot area; dashed threshold lines are described; gene labels explained as index-based.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is 54 KB with /FontDescriptor (TrueType embedding), /ToUnicode (selectable text), CIDFontType2 fonts, no Type3; scatter points rasterized per rcParams but axes/text/lines are vector.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png (300 dpi, 229 KB), figure.svg (122 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states specific numerical findings (235 up, 230 down, n=3000), defines both axes, explains color encoding, threshold values, and notes index-based gene IDs — complete standalone description.

strengths: Exemplary typography pipeline: fonttype=42/none set globally, Arial-first font stack, and automated pdffonts verification baked into the script.
Caption is unusually thorough — quantifies modularity, intra-edge fraction, layout parameters, and interprets the Q<0 finding in one concise sentence.
weaknesses: Q=-0.16 means the spring layout does not cluster same-community nodes together, so the figure's core visual message (community structure) is absent — the coloring is correct but the layout undermines it.

No node labels are drawn, which is fine for a hairball-avoidance brief, but combined with the non-clustered layout a reviewer may ask for a circular or community-grouped layout to make the coloring meaningful.

evidence.scientific_clarity: Brief asks for nodes colored by community with force-directed layout — delivered, but Q=-0.16 means spring layout scatters same-community nodes, so community structure is not visually apparent despite correct coloring.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' set in rcParams; font stack prioritizes Arial→Helvetica before DejaVu Sans fallback; legend at fontsize=8 is appropriate for 6×5 figure.
evidence.palette: ColorBrewer Set2 (4 colors) is colorblind-safe; used for categorical community labels only — no green/red directional misuse.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(6,5) with tight_layout(pad=0.3) and bbox_inches='tight'; edge alpha=0.30 avoids hairball; no node labels so no overlap risk; legend tucked to lower-left.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and legend; Q=-0.16 is explained; however, a reviewer may question why assigned communities have negative modularity and whether the data is meaningful for a community-coloring figure.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdf.fonttype=42 in rcParams; script runs pdffonts (with /Type3 byte-scan fallback) and exits non-zero on failure; SVG saved with svg.fonttype='none'; PDF is 19 KB (plausible vector size).
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (19K), figure.png (194K at 300 dpi), caption.tex (511B), gen_figure.py (7.3K) all present and non-trivial; bonus figure.svg also saved.
evidence.caption_quality: Specific numerical claims: Q=-0.16, 9% intra-community edges (6/69); explains ring-lattice topology as cause; documents layout seed, k, iterations, node-size mode, edge alpha, and color scheme.

strengths: High-quality LaTeX rendering: Type 1 (LM) fonts, booktabs rules, no vertical lines, subscript ±std notation, and group-spanning multicolumn header exactly match the brief's requirements.
Caption is exemplary — specific numerical claim (5.3 pp), bold convention explained, seed count stated, directly matching what a reviewer expects.
weaknesses: table.tex (standalone LaTeX source, explicitly required by the brief) is missing from the output directory, making the deliverable incomplete.

Minor: the within-1σ bolding logic in gen_figure.py applies the rule too broadly (it compares every method against the best, including the best itself, potentially double-counting), which could mislead in edge cases.

evidence.scientific_clarity: Booktabs table clearly presents 4 methods × 5 metrics with best-per-column bolding, group spans (Baselines / Ours), and upward-arrow metric labels; scientific claim is unambiguous.
evidence.typography: pdffonts shows all fonts are Type 1 (LM Sans / LM Roman / LM Math), fully embedded and subsetted — no Type 3 / bitmap fonts; sans-serif default (\sfdefault) matches publication style.
evidence.palette: Table has no color; black-and-white with bold text for emphasis is fully colorblind-safe and appropriate for a comparison table.
evidence.layout_density: 7 in × 3.5 in geometry fits the table content snugly; no excessive whitespace observed in the rasterized image; column widths are proportional.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption correctly explains bold convention and seed count; group spanning header (Baselines / Ours) is present; however table.tex (the standalone LaTeX source required by the brief) is absent from the output directory.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is a real pdfTeX-generated PDF 1.7 with fully embedded Type 1 fonts and selectable text; no Type 3 / bitmap fonts detected.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf and figure.png are present and non-trivial; caption.tex is present; gen_figure.py is present — but table.tex (explicitly required by the brief as a deliverable) is missing.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names all 4 methods, states 5 benchmarks, gives a concrete numerical claim ('up to 5.3 pp over strongest baseline on GPQA'), explains the bold convention, and reports seed count (5).

strengths: Exemplary LaTeX math typesetting: align environments, underbrace annotations, proper upright operators (KL, log, E), and consistent notation throughout all three equations.
Derivation narrative is self-contained and pedagogically clear — each step is motivated by prose that goes beyond a one-word justification.
weaknesses: Caption lacks any numerical claim or quantitative finding, which weakens its standalone informativeness for a journal figure.
Approximately 20% of the page is blank at the bottom; tightening vertical spacing or adding a brief summary remark would improve layout density.

evidence.scientific_clarity: Three-step derivation (ELBO → KL decomposition → retrieval-augmented objective) is clearly structured with bold step headers, underbrace labels, and explanatory prose that fully carries the RetroDiff training-objective claim.
evidence.typography: pdflatex with Computer Modern Type 1 fonts: inherently vector and selectable, no DejaVu Sans, appropriate title/body/math sizes; fonttype=42 is a matplotlib concept and does not apply to LaTeX-generated PDFs.
evidence.palette: No color used — trivially colorblind-safe and appropriate for a mathematical derivation block; underbrace labels provide categorical distinction via text.
evidence.layout_density: Content fills roughly 80% of the page with well-spaced equations and prose; noticeable blank whitespace at the bottom of the page reduces density.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption cross-references eq:elbo, eq:decomp, eq:retro matching the figure's equation labels exactly; prose is publication-quality and the figure needs no legend.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdflatex produces a real vector PDF (188 KB) with selectable Computer Modern Type 1 text; no raster elements embedded.
evidence.file_format: All four required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (188 KB), figure.png (514 KB), caption.tex (923 B), gen_figure.py (2.0 KB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains all three equations with correct mathematical terminology (Jensen's inequality, Markov factorisation, weighted ℓ2 loss, retrieval conditioning) but contains no specific numerical claim or quantitative finding.

strengths: Clean 5-panel workflow layout with tight margins and a prominent embedded headline finding satisfies the CNS graphical-abstract pattern; the 2:1 aspect and ≤4-hue palette are fully compliant.
PDF vector fidelity is exemplary: pdf.fonttype=42 enforced, all fonts embedded as CID TrueType with no Type-3 fallback, and the script self-verifies with pdffonts at render time.
weaknesses: Several text elements fall below the brief's 9pt minimum (noise labels at 6.5pt, tick labels at 7pt, legend at 7.5pt), and DejaVu Sans renders for math/italic text instead of Arial — both are typography regressions.
The diffusion-box denoising step labels (x_T … x_1) are decorative rather than informative at their rendered size, consuming panel space without adding scientific content a reviewer could read.

evidence.scientific_clarity: 5-panel workflow (Problem→Pipeline→3 results) conveys retrieval-augmented diffusion concept in <5 sec; headline '2× hit rate' embedded in figure centre; all three paper claims (hit rate, Vina score, convergence) are visually represented.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype=none correctly set; Arial renders for prose text; however DejaVu Sans falls back for math/italic elements (R², x_T noise labels) confirmed by pdffonts; several labels at 6.5–7.5pt violate the brief's ≥9pt minimum.
evidence.palette: Blue (#0F4D92) + orange (#D95F02) main pair is deuteranopia-safe; green (#1B7837) used exclusively for directional gain annotation (+18% bracket); red (#CC2222) confined to miss markers in Panel A with no red/green co-occurrence; ≤4 hues as required.
evidence.layout_density: Gridspec fills 96% of canvas width (left=0.02, right=0.98); 5 panels tile the 2:1 aspect well with no large voids; diffusion-box noise labels (6.5pt, 4 items) are cramped and risk being unreadable at print size.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches all figure panels; headline finding sentence ≤12 words embedded in figure; illustrative disclaimer present; legend in Panel D placed upper-right without overlap; no cross-references; self-contained.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts confirms CID TrueType fonts, all embedded and subsetted, zero Type-3 bitmap fonts; PDF is genuine vector at 62 KB; script includes pdffonts verification step with Type-3 detection fallback.
evidence.file_format: All four required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (62 KB), figure.png (344 KB, 3516×1841 px), caption.tex (472 B), gen_figure.py (17 KB); bonus figure.svg also produced.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states mechanism (ECFP4 retrieval → diffusion decoder) and lists four quantitative findings (2×, +18%, 60%, R²>0.9); includes illustrative disclaimer; slightly verbose for a graphical-abstract one-liner but all claims are specific and numerical.

strengths: Prose quality is high: five flowing paragraphs with no bullets, full STROBE items 4-12 coverage, and reproducibility detail (R 4.3.1, survival 3.5-7, mice 3.16.0, seed-equivalent via chained equations).
Vector fidelity is exemplary: Type 1 Latin Modern fonts fully embedded, selectable text confirmed via pdftotext, and gen_figure.py enforces a hard Type-3 rejection gate.
weaknesses: main.tex is missing from the output directory — the brief explicitly lists it as a required deliverable, so the submission is incomplete for reproducibility and editorial submission.

Several rubric axes (palette, typography fonttype=42) are designed for data figures and are not meaningfully applicable to a LaTeX-compiled prose document, reducing the discriminative value of those scores for this fixture type.

evidence.scientific_clarity: Five full-prose paragraphs cover all STROBE items 4-12 with specific numbers (n=1,987, κ=0.78, HR=1.5, 80% power, R 4.3.1); no bullets; claim-evidence-interpretation structure throughout.
evidence.typography: All fonts are Type 1 Latin Modern (LMRoman, LMMono, LMMathItalic), fully embedded and subset; no Type 3 bitmap fonts; no DejaVu Sans; standard LaTeX article sizing.
evidence.palette: Text-only document; no colour palette used, so no colorblind or directional/categorical violations are possible.
evidence.layout_density: Single A4 page, 124 KB PDF; five dense paragraphs fill the page with standard LaTeX margins; no gaping whitespace, no overlapping elements.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption explicitly states STROBE items 4-12 and analytic n=1,987 (revised per reviewer note); however main.tex — a required output per the brief — is absent from the working directory.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdfTeX-produced PDF with Type 1 embedded fonts; pdftotext extracts full text successfully; gen_figure.py explicitly checks for and rejects Type 3 fonts.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (124 KB), figure.png (637 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py are all present and non-trivial; main.tex is missing despite being a required deliverable in the brief.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states venue (Cell Reports Medicine), STROBE item range (4-12), analytic n (1,987), baseline n (2,412), loss to follow-up (17.6%), median follow-up (3.4 yr, IQR 2.6-4.1), primary method (Cox with time-varying covariates), imputation (m=10, mice 3.16.0), E-value, and IPW — multiple specific numerical claims.
